scraping_instructions.return_format: business_name, url_or_no_website_flag, review_count, rating, town, category, phone
scraping_instructions.do_not_enrich: BRAIN enriches on next run — HANDS returns raw scrape data only
scraping_instructions.ssl_check_required: YES — check each URL for HTTP vs HTTPS. Tag as ssl=true/false. This is the primary signal.
scraping_instructions.mobile_check: Secondary — Playwright screenshot or PageSpeed API check at mobile viewport
scraping_instructions.no_website_flag: If no website found via Google Maps listing, flag no_website=true

signal_assignment_note: No leads to assign signals to. All signal assignments await Batch 5 Ryedale raw data. When data arrives, BRAIN will run full waterfall.

waterfall_logic.confirmed_winner: ssl → PAS → first-name subject — 3/3 INTERESTED leads used this exact combination. Non-negotiable for Batch 5.

priority_1.notes: Use for all HTTP sites. SSL signal is the strongest pain-to-action trigger found in the campaign.

priority_2.expected_rate: Low (0% confirmed INTERESTED — but Tom Day-0 mobile email triggered Day-7 reply). Do not abandon — use as secondary when SSL absent.
priority_2.notes: If ssl=false AND mobile=true: use mobile PAS. Different angle from SSL — customers can't read on phone, lost trust signal.

priority_3.subject_pattern: [Business name] — [town] [trade]: customers can't find you online
priority_3.expected_rate: 0% confirmed in Batch 4. Lower bar for Ryedale: 4★+ AND 20+ reviews (was 50+ in Batch 4).
priority_3.notes: Ryedale trades may be more open to no-website outreach given rural, offline-referral economy. Reference their Google Maps listing specifically.

priority_4.subject_pattern: [First name] — [N] reviews but your site isn't ranking locally
priority_4.expected_rate: 0% confirmed. Pivot to KEYWORD GAP angle: 'customers searching X in Malton/Pickering can't find you' rather than abstract ranking claim.
priority_4.notes: Only for 4.5★+ with 100+ reviews. Reference the specific keyword gap: 'plumber Malton' or 'electrician Pickering'.
